title: Reorder and Resize Dictionary Table Columns
story: As a DataTools Pro user viewing the Object Dictionary, I want the table columns reordered so that Definition appears after Data Type and the column widths are more evenly distributed, so that the table is easier to scan and read.
description: The Dictionary table columns were reordered for improved readability. The new order places Definition after Data Type, followed by Metric Relation and Picklist Values. Column widths were adjusted for a cleaner, more evenly distributed layout. Because the column show/hide logic was index-based, the reordering also required updating the visibility rules to ensure the correct columns display for both the Dictionary and Migration views.
acceptance_criteria: Dictionary columns appear in the updated order with Definition after Data Type
Column widths are more evenly distributed for improved readability
Toggling between Dictionary and Migration shows the correct columns for each view
Column show/hide behavior is not broken by the reordering
ux_changes: Reordered Dictionary table columns (Definition after Data Type, then Metric Relation, then Picklist Values) and adjusted column widths for a cleaner layout.
implementation_notes: The column show/hide logic was index-based, so reordering required updating the visibility indices for both Dictionary and Migration views to prevent showing wrong columns in each context.
comment_summary: Developer rearranged column order and verified that show/hide behavior remained correct across Dictionary and Migration views.
